name: bladerf
title: bladerf
description:
Nuand bladeRF software-defined radio device (tools)
The Nuand bladeRF is an open-source software-defined radio (SDR) system,
comprised of an RF transceiver, a field-programmable gate array (FPGA),
a microcontroller driving a USB 3.0 interface, and a suite of host
libraries and drivers to facilitate interaction with the device.
.
This package provides command-line tools for controlling the bladeRF.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use bladeRF-cli because Nuand bladeRF software-defined radio device (tools).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use bladeRF-cli as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bladeRF-cli <options>` as documented upstream for bladeRF-cli.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bladeRF-cli -l hostedx40.rbf` as documented upstream for bladeRF-cli.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bladeRF-cli -f firmware.img` as documented upstream for bladeRF-cli.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair bladeRF-cli with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bladeRF-cli --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: bladeRF-cli - command line interface and test utility
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install bladerf (0.2024.05-1). Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `bladeRF-cli <options>`. Further invocations from upstream: `bladeRF-cli -l hostedx40.rbf`; `bladeRF-cli -f firmware.img`. Nuand bladeRF software-defined radio device (tools).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Nuand bladeRF software-defined radio device (tools).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: bladeRF-cli - command line interface and test utility.
